narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing you'll likely struggle with the bar exam feels like a gut punch, especially with your full-time commitment and that Native American heritage you're proud of. Our prediction model looked at your story holistically and saw a 69.8% chance of not passing the bar, painting a picture of someone whose path might hit tough roadblocks despite your upper-middle family support. The biggest signal came from your Year 3 decile of 1, placing you at the very bottom of your class then, which screamed the strongest warning about future exam readiness. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 29.5, well below what typically lights the way to bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 2 wasn't much brighter, reinforcing that early struggles carried forward. Even with your family's upper-middle income, which usually offers a safety net, it didn't outweigh these academic flags in the model's eyes. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.4 added another layer of concern, suggesting foundational challenges that might persist. As a full-time male student with your racial background, these details together tipped the scales toward failure more than they'd help. It's not that any one thing dooms you—it's how they combine to tell a story of consistent hurdles in competitive academic arenas like the bar. This isn't the end of your journey; many have turned similar profiles around with targeted prep. What if you revisited those study habits from tougher years or boosted that LSAT foundation? You've got resilience in your story—use it to rewrite the next chapter. We're rooting for you.
